title: Calling Grandma
summary: A young girl calls her grandmother on the phone for the very first time by herself. They talk about her day, grandma's garden, and exciting plans to visit soon.

body.speaker: Grandma
body.text: Emma! Oh, how lovely! Did you call me all by yourself?

body.speaker: Emma
body.text: Yes! Mum showed me how to find your number. I wanted to talk to you.

body.speaker: Grandma
body.text: I'm so happy you called, sweetheart. How was your day?

body.speaker: Emma
body.text: It was really good! We painted pictures at school today. I painted a rainbow.

body.speaker: Grandma
body.text: A rainbow! That sounds beautiful. What colours did you use?

body.speaker: Emma
body.text: Red, orange, yellow, green, blue, and purple! My teacher put it on the wall.

body.speaker: Grandma
body.text: Well done! I'm very proud of you. How is your little brother?

body.speaker: Emma
body.text: He's okay. He has a cold. Grandma, what did you do today?

body.speaker: Grandma
body.text: I was in the garden this morning. My roses are blooming and they smell wonderful.

body.speaker: Emma
body.text: I love your garden! Grandma, can we come and visit you soon?

body.speaker: Grandma
body.text: Of course! I would love that. Maybe next weekend — if the weather is nice.

body.speaker: Emma
body.text: Yes please! I can help you in the garden. I know how to water the plants now.

body.speaker: Grandma
body.text: Then I will save some watering for you! I can't wait to see you, darling.

body.speaker: Emma
body.text: Me too, Grandma. I love you. Bye bye!

body.speaker: Grandma
body.text: Love you too, my darling. Bye bye!

annotations.meaningVi: hoàn toàn một mình, tự làm
annotations.exampleEn: She tied her shoes all by herself for the first time.

annotations.meaningVi: người thân yêu (cách gọi thân mật)
annotations.exampleEn: 'Good night, sweetheart,' said the mother to her child.
After the rain, we saw a beautiful rainbow in the sky.
The cherry trees are blooming pink in spring.
'Well done! You got full marks!' said the teacher.
Her parents were very proud of her results.
He can't come to school today because he has a cold.
She waters the plants every evening after dinner.
Can we meet next weekend for coffee?

annotations.meaningVi: tôi không thể chờ được, tôi rất háo hức
annotations.exampleEn: I can't wait for the school holidays to start!
'Come here, darling,' said the grandmother with open arms.

annotations.meaningVi: treo lên tường, dán lên tường
annotations.exampleEn: The teacher put the best artwork on the wall for everyone to see.

grammarNotes.title: Simple Past for Telling About Your Day
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Khi kể chuyện về ngày của mình, chúng ta dùng thì quá khứ đơn: 'We painted pictures.' / 'My teacher put it on the wall.' / 'I was in the garden this morning.' Đây là cách tự nhiên và thông dụng nhất để kể lại những gì đã xảy ra trong ngày hôm đó.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: When talking about what happened during the day, we use the simple past: 'We painted pictures.' / 'My teacher put it on the wall.' / 'I was in the garden.' This is the natural tense for sharing news about your day in conversation.

grammarNotes.title: Modal 'Can' for Ability
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Can' diễn tả khả năng — điều mà ai đó biết làm hoặc có thể làm. Ví dụ: 'I know how to water the plants now.' và 'I can help you in the garden.' Câu 'Can we come and visit you?' dùng 'can' để hỏi xin phép một cách nhẹ nhàng.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Can' expresses ability — something someone is able to do. 'I can help you in the garden.' / 'I know how to water the plants now.' 'Can we come and visit?' uses 'can' to make a friendly request or ask permission. It is more informal than 'may we'.

translationVi:
Emma: Alo? Bà ơi, con đây — Emma!
Bà: Emma! Ôi, vui quá! Con tự gọi cho bà một mình à?
Emma: Vâng ạ! Mẹ chỉ con cách tìm số của bà. Con muốn nói chuyện với bà.
Bà: Bà rất vui vì con gọi, cưng ơi. Hôm nay của con thế nào?
Emma: Rất vui ạ! Hôm nay ở trường chúng con được vẽ tranh. Con vẽ một cái cầu vồng.
Bà: Cầu vồng! Nghe đẹp quá nhỉ. Con dùng những màu gì vậy?
Emma: Đỏ, cam, vàng, xanh lá, xanh dương và tím ạ! Cô giáo treo tranh của con lên tường rồi.
Bà: Giỏi lắm! Bà rất tự hào về con. Em trai con thế nào rồi?
Emma: Em ổn ạ. Em bị cảm lạnh. Bà ơi, hôm nay bà làm gì vậy?
Bà: Sáng nay bà ở trong vườn. Những bông hồng của bà đang nở và thơm lắm.
Emma: Con thích vườn của bà lắm! Bà ơi, chúng con có thể đến thăm bà sớm không ạ?
Bà: Được chứ! Bà rất muốn vậy. Có thể cuối tuần tới — nếu trời đẹp.
Emma: Vâng ạ! Con có thể giúp bà trong vườn. Con đã biết tưới cây rồi.
Bà: Thế thì bà sẽ dành phần tưới cây cho con nhé! Bà không thể chờ được để gặp con, cưng ơi.
Emma: Con cũng vậy, bà ơi. Con yêu bà. Tạm biệt bà!
Bà: Bà cũng yêu con, cưng của bà. Tạm biệt con!

questions.prompt: Who taught Emma how to find her grandmother's number?
questions.explanation: Emma says 'Mum showed me how to find your number.'
questions.hint: Who helped Emma make the call?

questions.prompt: What did Emma paint at school?
questions.hint: What colourful subject did Emma choose for her picture?

questions.prompt: How many colours did Emma use in her painting?
questions.explanation: Emma lists: red, orange, yellow, green, blue, and purple — that is six colours.

questions.prompt: What did the teacher do with Emma's painting?
questions.explanation: Emma says 'My teacher put it on the wall.'
questions.hint: Where did the teacher display Emma's work?

questions.explanation: Emma says 'He's okay. He has a cold.'
questions.hint: What does Emma say about her brother's health?

questions.prompt: What was Grandma doing this morning?
questions.explanation: Grandma says 'I was in the garden this morning.'
questions.hint: Where was Grandma spending her morning?

questions.prompt: What does Grandma say about her roses?
options: They are blooming and smell wonderful
They are very small this year
questions.explanation: Grandma says 'My roses are blooming and they smell wonderful.'
questions.hint: What two things does Grandma say about her roses?

questions.prompt: When does Grandma suggest they visit?
questions.explanation: Grandma says 'Maybe next weekend — if the weather is nice.'
questions.hint: What time does Grandma suggest for the visit?

questions.prompt: What condition does Grandma add to the visit plan?
questions.explanation: Grandma says 'Maybe next weekend — if the weather is nice.'
questions.hint: What does Grandma say needs to be good for the visit to happen?

questions.prompt: How does Emma say she can help Grandma in the garden?
questions.explanation: Emma says 'I know how to water the plants now.'
questions.hint: What garden skill has Emma recently learned?

questions.prompt: This is a special call for Emma because she:
options: Spoke to Grandma in another language
Made the call all by herself for the first time
questions.explanation: Grandma asks 'Did you call me all by yourself?' and Emma confirms she did.
questions.hint: Why is this phone call an achievement for Emma?

cloze.template:
Emma: Hello? Grandma, it's me — Emma!
Grandma: Emma! Oh, how lovely! Did you call me all by {{b1}}?
Emma: Yes! Mum showed me how to find your number. I wanted to talk to you.
Grandma: I'm so happy you called, sweetheart. How was your {{b2}}?
Emma: It was really good! We painted pictures at school today. I painted a {{b3}}.
Grandma: A rainbow! That sounds beautiful. What colours did you use?
Emma: Red, orange, yellow, green, blue, and purple! My teacher put it on the {{b4}}.
Grandma: Well done! I'm very {{b5}} of you. How is your little brother?
Emma: He's okay. He has a {{b6}}. Grandma, what did you do today?
Grandma: I was in the garden this morning. My roses are {{b7}} and they smell wonderful.
Emma: I love your garden! Grandma, can we come and visit you {{b8}}?
Grandma: Of course! I would love that. Maybe next weekend — if the {{b9}} is nice.
Emma: Yes please! I can help you in the garden. I know how to {{b10}} the plants now.
Grandma: Then I will save some watering for you! I can't wait to see you, darling.
Emma: Me too, Grandma. I love you. Bye bye!
Grandma: Love you too, my darling. Bye bye!

blanks.explanation: 'All by yourself' means without anyone's help — a phrase showing independence.
'How was your day?' is the most natural way to ask someone what happened today.
Emma painted 'a rainbow' — a colourful arc seen in the sky after rain.
Teachers often display good artwork by putting it on the classroom 'wall'.
'Proud of you' means feeling great respect and admiration for what someone has done.
'Has a cold' is a common phrase meaning suffering from a minor illness with a runny nose.
'Blooming' means the flowers are fully open and at their most beautiful.
'Visit you soon' means she wants to come in the near future — not a distant promise.
'If the weather is nice' — a common condition for outdoor plans in countries with variable weather.
'Water the plants' means to give plants water so they can grow.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Emma calls her grandmother by herself for the first time, and they have a warm, happy conversation. In a world of texting and video calls, do you think phone calls are still important? What is special about hearing someone's voice rather than reading their words?
